description_long: This is an old, rectangular glass bottle with a narrow neck and a short cylindrical cap. The bottle's surface is heavily weathered, showing significant discoloration and a crusty, whitish residue, likely from age or exposure to the elements. The glass appears to be amber or brown in color, with some areas more translucent than others due to surface deposits. Embossed text is faintly visible on at least two sides, suggesting it may have once contained a branded product, possibly medicinal or alcoholic. The bottle is empty, and its base is flat, allowing it to stand upright. The overall appearance suggests it has been discarded or left outdoors for a long period, giving it a distinctly aged and urban-found look.
description: A weathered, rectangular amber glass bottle with a short neck and visible surface residue, featuring faint embossed text and a flat base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bottle's rectangular face with faint embossed text and heavy surface residue.
description_view_1: Side view reveals more of the bottle's weathered texture and additional embossed text, with the neck and cap clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite face displays another panel with faint text and a similar crusty, aged appearance.
description_view_3: Rear view shows the plainest side, mostly covered in discoloration and residue, with no visible text.
attribute_label_or_branding: Faint embossed text visible on two sides, but specific wording is unclear due to weathering and residue.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Heavy surface discoloration, crusty residue, and signs of weathering; no visible cracks or major deformations.
